title: Orrery
source: location-framing.md → Volume: Orrery (anchor orrery); component detail + base mechanism probed in-game via tools/play.cjs
frame: +X east, +Z south, +Y up. Wide TALL six-sided chamber. From the entry (east) the brass disk reads as a clean free-spinning PERFECT CIRCLE on a tilted axis (~4-5deg), lifted clear of the floor. ALL the machinery (gear drive, cogs, axle pylon) is tucked BEHIND the disk (west) so the disk occludes it from the front — glimpsed only at the disk's edges, in the gap below it, and from the alcove side-views. Only the disk + the proud, free-spinning sub-wheels (carrying the orbs) are on the front face.

parts.name: ceiling
parts.detail: high roof; the vault above the machine is lost in shadow

parts.name: rear axle pylon (hidden behind disk)
parts.detail: support pylon BEHIND the disk; carries the tilted axle to the hub. Occluded by the disk from the front.

parts.name: axle shaft to hub
parts.detail: tilted axle from the rear pylon into the disk's hub (behind the disk)

parts.name: gear drive base (behind disk)
parts.detail: the engine of gears/shafts, tucked BEHIND and below the disk; only glimpsed in the gap under the disk and from the side. Render gearwork richly here.

parts.name: drive cog south (behind)
parts.detail: large brass cog behind the disk, driving its rim

parts.name: drive cog north (behind)
parts.detail: large brass cog behind the disk on the other side

parts.name: drive cog small (behind)
parts.detail: smaller idler cog in the drive train, behind the disk

parts.name: orrery disk
parts.detail: the great brass disk: a free-spinning clean PERFECT CIRCLE on a tilted axis, lifted clear of the floor, full rim showing; engraved with arcs/lines; turns about the fixed central hub globe. Reads as a clean circle from the front — machinery is behind it.

parts.name: blue-glass globe (centre/hub, fixed)
parts.detail: large fixed blue-glass globe at the disk's hub (brown/white island-map pattern); the disk rotates around it

parts.name: silver wheel spindle
parts.detail: short spindle standing the silver wheel proud of the disk face so it turns freely in front of it

parts.name: silver wheel (upper side)
parts.detail: SMALL silver sub-wheel proud of the disk face (much smaller than the great disk); turns freely, carrying the tan globe around the violet hub

parts.name: violet globe (silver wheel hub)
parts.detail: violet globe at the silver wheel's CENTRE — the tan globe orbits around THIS one

parts.name: silver wheel arm (hub to rim)
parts.detail: single radial arm carrying the tan globe around the violet hub — angled so the orbit reads from the front

parts.name: tan globe (silver wheel rim)
parts.detail: small tan globe on the end of the silver wheel's arm, freely circling the violet hub; ringed with frosted white glass (blue mask caught in the rings)

parts.name: copper wheel spindle
parts.detail: short spindle standing the copper wheel proud of the disk face

parts.name: copper wheel (lower side)
parts.detail: SMALL copper sub-wheel proud of the disk face (much smaller than the great disk); turns freely, carrying the green globe around the red hub

parts.name: red globe (copper wheel hub)
parts.detail: red globe at the copper wheel's CENTRE — the green globe orbits around THIS one

parts.name: copper wheel arm (hub to rim)
parts.detail: single radial arm carrying the green globe around the red hub

parts.name: green globe (copper wheel rim)
parts.detail: small pocked green globe on the end of the copper wheel's arm, freely circling the red hub

parts.name: dangling grey strip
parts.detail: thin grey RIBBON of soft cloth-like material hanging limp from the dark vault over the disk's upper edge — out of reach; a soft dangling strip, not a rigid pole

parts.name: south alcove floor
parts.detail: small curved alcove off the south side (deepened back so the camera fits inside)

parts.name: south alcove ladder rail front
parts.detail: iron ladder up a shaft, mounted flat against the WEST wall of the south alcove — on the viewer's LEFT when standing in the alcove looking north out toward the orrery (→ Starry Dome North)

parts.name: north alcove floor
parts.detail: small curved alcove off the north side (deepened back to set the stairs behind an archway)

parts.name: north stair archway lintel
parts.detail: stone ARCHWAY at the back of the north alcove framing the ascending stairs beyond it (→ Curving Hall West End); the flight rises up through it and out of view

parts.name: north alcove stairs step 1
parts.detail: front (lowest) tread of a BIG ascending stone staircase filling the north archway, rising away to the north and up out of view (→ Curving Hall West End). Modelled as real rising steps (not a single block) so the flight reads as a large extending staircase both up-close from the north alcove AND seen small across the whole chamber from the south alcove.

parts.name: north alcove stairs step 8
parts.detail: topmost treads climb up past the top of the frame — the flight continues out of view (→ Curving Hall West End)

parts.name: north alcove ceiling
parts.detail: ceiling covers only the FRONT (chamber side) of the alcove; the back is left OPEN as a stair shaft so the ascending flight climbs up out of view (→ Curving Hall West End)

notes.orrery: DIM, deeply shadowed chamber — faint light, brass catching low warm highlights, high vault lost in blackness. From the entry the great disk reads as a CLEAN, free-spinning PERFECT CIRCLE turning on its central hub, lifted clear of the floor, caught MID-ROTATION (in motion, NOT a fixed dial/plaque/astrolabe). ALL the gear machinery is tucked BEHIND the disk — render rich gears/shafts glimpsed at the disk's edges, in the gap beneath it, and not breaking the clean circular silhouette. The two sub-wheels stand PROUD of the disk's face and turn FREELY, each carrying its globe around a hub (tan around violet; green around red) — not flat inlays. Blue hub globe fixed at centre. The sub-wheels are SMALL relative to the great disk (do not let them read as a second competing circle). A thin grey RIBBON/cord of soft cloth-like material hangs limp from the dark vault, dangling over the disk's upper edge, out of reach — a soft hanging strip, NOT a rigid pole, axle or strut. Eye-level, pressed back against the entry (east) wall, the disk filling the frame.
notes.south-alcove: Stand in the small alcove looking through the opening at the SAME great machine turning in the chamber, seen three-quarter from this side; its gearwork visible. A runged iron LADDER climbs a shaft against the WEST wall — on the LEFT of the frame. Far across the chamber, in the north alcove, a stone ARCHWAY frames a big ascending STAIRCASE rising up out of sight — small and distant, straight ahead. DIM, deeply shadowed — faint light, brass catching low warm highlights.
